title: Khatu shyam ji Chalisa
intro: Shri Khatu Shyam Chalisa - Hymn to Khatu Shyam
description:
॥ Shri Khatu Shyam Chalisa (English Transliteration) ॥

॥ Doha ॥
Shri guru charanan dhyan dhar, sumir sachchidanand.
Shyam chalisa banat hai, rach chaupai chhand.

॥ Chaupai ॥
Shyam-Shyam bhaji barambara.
Sahaj hi ho bhavsagar para.
In sam dev na duja koi.
Din dayalu na data hoi.
Bheem suputra Ahilavati jaya.
Kahi Bheem ka pautra kahalaya.
Yah sab katha kahi kalpantar.
Tanik na mano ismein antar.
Barbarik Vishnu avtara.
Bhaktan hetu manuj tan dhara.
Basudev Devki pyare.
Jasumati maiya Nand dulare.
Madhusudan Gopal Murari.
Vrajkishor Govardhan dhari.
Siyaram Shri Hari Govinda.
Dinpal Shri Bal Mukunda.
Damodar ran chhod Bihari.
Nath Dwarkadhish Kharari.
Radhaballabh Rukmani kanta.
Gopi ballabh Kans hananta.
Manmohan chit chor kahaye.
Makhan chori-chari kar khaye.
Murlidhar Yadupati Ghanshyama.
Krishna patit pavan abhirama.
Mayapati Lakshmipati Isha.
Purushottam Keshav Jagdisha.
Vishwapati jai bhuvan pasara.
Dinbandhu bhaktan rakhvara.
Prabhu ka bhed na koi paya.
Shesh Mahesh thake muniraya.
Narad Sharad rishi yogindar.
Shyam-Shyam sab ratat nirantar.
Kavi kodi kari kanan ginanta.
Naam apar athah ananta.
Har srishti har sug mein bhai.
Ye avtar bhakt sukhdai.
Hraday mahi kari dekhu vichara.
Shyam bhaje to ho nistara.
Kaur padhavat ganika tari.
Bhilni ki bhakti balihari.
Sati Ahilya Gautam nari.
Bhai shrapvash shila dulari.
Shyam charan raj chit layi.
Pahunchi pati lok mein jahi.
Ajamil aru Sadan kasai.
Naam pratap param gati pai.
Jake Shyam naam adhara.
Sukh lahahi dukh door ho sara.
Shyam salovan hai ati sundar.
Mor mukut sir tan pitambar.
Gale vaijanti maal suhai.
Chhavi anoop bhaktan maan bhai.
Shyam-Shyam sumirahu din-rati.
Shyam dupahari kar parbhati.
Shyam sarathi jis rath ke.
Rode door hoye us path ke.
Shyam bhakt na kahi par hara.
Bheer pari tab Shyam pukara.
Rasana Shyam naam ras pee le.
Jee le Shyam naam ke hi le.
Sansari sukh bhog milega.
Ant Shyam sukh yog milega.
Shyam Prabhu hain tan ke kale.
Man ke gore bhole-bhale.
Shyam sant bhaktan hitkari.
Rog-dosh adh nashe bhari.
Prem sahit jab naam pukara.
Bhakt lagat Shyam ko pyara.
Khatu mein hain Mathuravasi.
Parbrahm purna avinashi.
Sudha taan bhari murli bajai.
Chahu dishi jahan suni pai.
Vriddh-baal jete nari nar.
Mugdh bhaye suni banshi swar.
Hadbad kar sab pahunche jai.
Khatu mein jahan Shyam Kanhai.
Jisne Shyam swaroop nihara.
Bhav bhay se paya chhutkara.

॥ Doha ॥
Shyam salone sanware, Barbarik tanudhar.
Ichha purna bhakt ki, karo na lao baar.
meaning:
॥ Shri Khatu Shyam Chalisa (English Transliteration & Meaning) ॥

॥ Doha ॥
Shri guru charanan dhyan dhar, sumir sachchidanand.
(Meditating on the feet of the Guru and remembering the Truth-Consciousness-Bliss, the Supreme Being,)
Shyam chalisa banat hai, rach chaupai chhand.
(I compose this Shyam Chalisa, arranged in the metre of Chaupai.)

॥ Chaupai ॥
Shyam-Shyam bhaji barambara.
(Chant "Shyam-Shyam" again and again.)
Sahaj hi ho bhavsagar para.
(You will easily cross the ocean of worldly existence.)
In sam dev na duja koi.
(There is no other deity equal to Him.)
Din dayalu na data hoi.
(There is no other giver or merciful one to the distressed like Him.)
Bheem suputra Ahilavati jaya.
(You are the son of Bheem and Ahilavati.)
Kahi Bheem ka pautra kahalaya.
(Thus, you are called the grandson of Bheem.)
Yah sab katha kahi kalpantar.
(This story has been told through the ages.)
Tanik na mano ismein antar.
(Do not doubt this in the slightest.)
Barbarik Vishnu avtara.
(You, Barbarik, are an incarnation of Vishnu.)
Bhaktan hetu manuj tan dhara.
(You took a human form for the sake of your devotees.)
Basudev Devki pyare.
(You are dear to Vasudeva and Devaki.)
Jasumati maiya Nand dulare.
(You are the beloved of Mother Yashoda and Nanda Baba.)
Madhusudan Gopal Murari.
(You are Madhusudan, Gopal, and Murari.)
Vrajkishor Govardhan dhari.
(You are the youth of Vraj and the lifter of Mount Govardhan.)
Siyaram Shri Hari Govinda.
(You are Siyaram, Shri Hari, and Govinda.)
Dinpal Shri Bal Mukunda.
(You are the protector of the poor and the child Mukunda.)
Damodar ran chhod Bihari.
(You are Damodar, the one who left the battlefield, and Bihari.)
Nath Dwarkadhish Kharari.
(You are the Lord, Dwarkadhish, and the destroyer of foes.)
Radhaballabh Rukmani kanta.
(You are the beloved of Radha and the husband of Rukmini.)
Gopi ballabh Kans hananta.
(You are the beloved of the Gopis and the destroyer of Kansa.)
Manmohan chit chor kahaye.
(You are called Manmohan and the stealer of hearts.)
Makhan chori-chari kar khaye.
(You stole and ate butter.)
Murlidhar Yadupati Ghanshyama.
(You are the flute-bearer, Lord of the Yadus, and Ghanshyam.)
Krishna patit pavan abhirama.
(You are Krishna, the purifier of the fallen, and ever-beautiful.)
Mayapati Lakshmipati Isha.
(You are the Lord of Maya, Lord of Lakshmi, and the Supreme God.)
Purushottam Keshav Jagdisha.
(You are the Supreme Man, Keshav, and the Lord of the Universe.)
Vishwapati jai bhuvan pasara.
(You are the Lord of the world, and your realm is vast.)
Dinbandhu bhaktan rakhvara.
(You are a friend to the distressed and a protector of devotees.)
Prabhu ka bhed na koi paya.
(No one has been able to fathom the mystery of the Lord.)
Shesh Mahesh thake muniraya.
(Even Sheshnag, Lord Shiva, and great sages were exhausted trying.)
Narad Sharad rishi yogindar.
(Narada, Saraswati, and great sages/yogis.)
Shyam-Shyam sab ratat nirantar.
(All are constantly chanting "Shyam-Shyam".)
Kavi kodi kari kanan ginanta.
(Poets and wise men count your virtues.)
Naam apar athah ananta.
(Your names are boundless, unfathomable, and infinite.)
Har srishti har sug mein bhai.
(In every creation and every age, O Brother,)
Ye avtar bhakt sukhdai.
(This incarnation is the giver of happiness to devotees.)
Hraday mahi kari dekhu vichara.
(Reflect upon this within your heart.)
Shyam bhaje to ho nistara.
(If you worship Shyam, you will attain salvation.)
Kaur padhavat ganika tari.
(You saved the Ganika who read/taught the scriptures.)
Bhilni ki bhakti balihari.
(The devotion of the Bhilni (Shabari) is glorious.)
Sati Ahilya Gautam nari.
(Sati Ahilya, the wife of Gautam Rishi.)
Bhai shrapvash shila dulari.
(Became a beloved stone due to a curse.)
Shyam charan raj chit layi.
(By placing the dust of Shyam's feet in her heart,)
Pahunchi pati lok mein jahi.
(She reached her husband's realm.)
Ajamil aru Sadan kasai.
(Ajamil and Sadan the butcher also,)
Naam pratap param gati pai.
(Attained the highest state through the power of your name.)
Jake Shyam naam adhara.
(For those who have the support of Shyam's name,)
Sukh lahahi dukh door ho sara.
(They attain happiness and all their sorrows vanish.)
Shyam salovan hai ati sundar.
(Shyam's dark form is extremely beautiful.)
Mor mukut sir tan pitambar.
(With a peacock crown on his head and a yellow garment on his body.)
Gale vaijanti maal suhai.
(The Vaijayanti garland looks beautiful around his neck.)
Chhavi anoop bhaktan maan bhai.
(This incomparable image pleases the minds of devotees.)
Shyam-Shyam sumirahu din-rati.
(Remember Shyam-Shyam day and night.)
Shyam dupahari kar parbhati.
(Remember Shyam in the afternoon and the morning.)
Shyam sarathi jis rath ke.
(The chariot for which Shyam is the charioteer,)
Rode door hoye us path ke.
(The obstacles in its path are cleared away.)
Shyam bhakt na kahi par hara.
(A devotee of Shyam is never defeated anywhere.)
Bheer pari tab Shyam pukara.
(When in trouble, one calls out to Shyam.)
Rasana Shyam naam ras pee le.
(Drink the nectar of Shyam's name with your tongue.)
Jee le Shyam naam ke hi le.
(Live your life relying only on Shyam's name.)
Sansari sukh bhog milega.
(You will receive worldly pleasures.)
Ant Shyam sukh yog milega.
(And in the end, you will attain union with Shyam.)
Shyam Prabhu hain tan ke kale.
(Lord Shyam is dark-skinned.)
Man ke gore bhole-bhale.
(But he is pure-hearted and innocent.)
Shyam sant bhaktan hitkari.
(Shyam is the well-wisher of saints and devotees.)
Rog-dosh adh nashe bhari.
(He destroys major diseases, faults, and sins.)
Prem sahit jab naam pukara.
(When called upon with love,)
Bhakt lagat Shyam ko pyara.
(The devotee becomes dear to Shyam.)
Khatu mein hain Mathuravasi.
(The dweller of Mathura (Shyam) is in Khatu.)
Parbrahm purna avinashi.
(He is the Supreme Brahman, complete and imperishable.)
Sudha taan bhari murli bajai.
(He played the flute filled with the nectar of melody.)
Chahu dishi jahan suni pai.
(Which was heard in all four directions.)
Vriddh-baal jete nari nar.
(All elderly, children, women, and men.)
Mugdh bhaye suni banshi swar.
(Were mesmerized hearing the sound of the flute.)
Hadbad kar sab pahunche jai.
(Hurriedly, everyone reached the place,)
Khatu mein jahan Shyam Kanhai.
(In Khatu where Shyam Kanhai resides.)
Jisne Shyam swaroop nihara.
(Whoever has looked upon the form of Shyam,)
Bhav bhay se paya chhutkara.
(Has been liberated from the fear of worldly existence.)

॥ Doha ॥
Shyam salone sanware, Barbarik tanudhar.
(O beautiful dark-complexioned Shyam, bearer of the Barbarik form,)
Ichha purna bhakt ki, karo na lao baar.
(Fulfill the wishes of your devotee, do not delay.)

faq.q1: What is the greatest benefit of the Khatu Shyam Chalisa?
faq.a1: It is known as the 'support of the defeated.' Reciting it provides immense mental strength, peace, and confidence to the devotee during difficult times.
faq.q2: Does reading it help resolve difficult situations or failed tasks?
faq.a2: Yes, Baba Shyam is believed to take away the troubles of his devotees. Reciting the Chalisa with full faith is believed to remove obstacles from your path.
faq.q3: Is it necessary to visit a temple to read it?
faq.a3: No, you can recite it at your home temple with full devotion and purity.
faq.q4: Is there a specific day or time to read it?
faq.a4: Reciting it on Ekadashi or Saturdays is considered highly fruitful, though you can recite it on any day according to your convenience.
faq.q5: Can devotees read it from a mobile device?
faq.a5: Yes, in devotion, your 'Bhav' (sincere feeling) is more important than the medium you use (mobile or book).
